input: {"questions":[{"question":"The live spec shows the public API splits into three tiers: (1) anonymous reads — resolve, artist, bandcamp(+bulk), search, breakouts, tastemakers, dossier/{grain}/{key}, root index; (2) key-gated reads that already 401/402 — masters, facets, usage, wayfind/interpret; (3) beacon-JWT endpoints — search-events/*. The handoff's V1 sketch only commits to the four conveniences. How wide should V1's typed surface be? (Error handling for 401/402/404/413 is universal in all options; this only scopes which methods ship.)","header":"V1 surface","options":[{"label":"Conveniences + anon reads","description":"Recommended. Ship artist/resolve/bandcamp(+bulk) [P0] + thin typed wrappers for the other ANONYMOUS reads (search, breakouts, tastemakers, ones-to-watch, dossier/{grain}/{key}, root index, manifest). Defer key-gated (masters/facets/usage/wayfind-interpret) + beacon to a post-keys minor. The Stripe/Supabase 'medium' sweet spot; nothing that needs a key we don't have yet."},{"label":"Core conveniences only","description":"Leanest, most literal to the handoff sketch: just artist/resolve/bandcamp(+bulk) + typed errors/retry/optional key. Everything else (incl. anon reads like search/tastemakers) deferred."},{"label":"Full public surface","description":"Ship typed methods for ALL public endpoints incl. key-gated ones (surface 401/402 when keyless) and the beacon/JWT search-events flow. Most complete, most work; beacon needs a distinct auth flow and can't be fully tested until the key/beacon model lands."}],"multiSelect":false}]}

{"questions":[{"question":"The spec declares info.license = 'Commercial — see Terms of Service' (crate is a paid API). What should the @hosaka/crate CLIENT package ship under? (This only blocks flipping the publish gate, which is off until keys land — it does not block the build, and it's a one-field change later.)","header":"License","options":[{"label":"MIT (Recommended)","description":"Ecosystem norm for official clients of paid APIs — stripe-node and @supabase/supabase-js are both MIT. The client code is permissive; paid access stays gated by crate's terms + API key. Easiest adoption."},{"label":"Apache-2.0","description":"Permissive like MIT but with an explicit patent grant (openai-node uses this). Same practical effect for consumers; slightly more enterprise-friendly."},{"label":"UNLICENSED / proprietary","description":"Strictly mirror the spec's 'Commercial' declaration — ship a proprietary LICENSE and keep the client itself closed. Most conservative legally."}],"multiSelect":false},{"question":"How should crate.artist('Aphex Twin') behave for a plain name (not a 64-hex cluster_id and not a discogs:/mbid: locator)?","header":"artist() DX","options":[{"label":"Direct pass-through (Recommended)","description":"One call: GET /artist/{key}. The endpoint name-resolves natively and the returned dossier already carries resolved_via/identity. Fewer round-trips, simplest."},{"label":"Resolve-first","description":"Two calls: GET /resolve then GET /artist/{cluster_id}. Surfaces IdentityResolution (resolved_via/matched_on/note) explicitly before fetching, at the cost of an extra round-trip."}],"multiSelect":false}]}

{"questions":[{"question":"The work is committed on feat/crate-sdk-v1-cycle-001 (clean tree). Publishing stays gated off regardless. What should I do to finish?","header":"Next step","options":[{"label":"Push + draft PR","description":"Push the branch and open a draft PR against main so the cycle is reviewable on GitHub (outward-facing; no publish)."},{"label":"Tag v0.1.0 in-repo","description":"Create the annotated v0.1.0 tag locally (no push) — the 'build + tag in-repo' deliverable from the locked decisions."},{"label":"Leave as-is on branch","description":"Stop here; the 4 commits stay local on the feature branch for you to handle."}],"multiSelect":true}]}

{"questions":[{"question":"How do you want to get the first (keyed) user onto the SDK? The keyed path works on the current build today.","header":"First user","options":[{"label":"Branch install now (Recommended)","description":"I prep a git-install one-liner + a packed tarball + a 2-line keyed quickstart for jani's friend. Works today with their key, no npm publish, no cycle-C dependency. Public npm release follows as key-first 0.2.0."},{"label":"Publish 0.1.0 to npm now","description":"Flip the publish gate and ship 0.1.0 now. Fastest install UX, but it doesn't enforce key-first yet — fine for the invited keyed user, risky for any keyless public installer at cycle-C. Needs jani's green-light + NPM_TOKEN."},{"label":"Wait for 0.2.0","description":"Hold the first user a few days until cycle-C/1.1.0 deploys, then publish the clean key-first 0.2.0 to npm as the single launch."}],"multiSelect":false},{"question":"Should I stage the 0.2.0 key-first changes now, or wait until spec 1.1.0 actually deploys?","header":"0.2.0 timing","options":[{"label":"Stage now (Recommended)","description":"Implement key-first guard + docs + X-RateLimit surfacing now against the announced 1.1.0 shape, so the moment cycle-C deploys it's just `regenerate types + re-gate + publish`. Some rework risk if the spec lands differently than described."},{"label":"Wait for 1.1.0","description":"Do nothing until cycle-C deploys, then run the full 0.2.0 cycle in one go against the real 1.1.0 spec. Zero rework, but the work happens after deploy (a bit slower to ship 0.2.0)."}],"multiSelect":false}]}

{"questions":[{"question":"How do you want to proceed to npm publish 0.2.0? (You'll add the NPM_TOKEN secret either way — I can't handle secrets.)","header":"Publish","options":[{"label":"Prep repo for CI publish (Recommended)","description":"I merge PR #1 → main, remove private:true, and push the v0.2.0 tag. You add NPM_TOKEN + set PUBLISH_ENABLED=true, and release.yml publishes on the tag. Clean, with CI provenance."},{"label":"Prep, you publish manually","description":"I merge PR #1 → main + remove private:true. You run `npm publish --access public` locally with your token in env. Fastest, no secret wrangling in GitHub."},{"label":"Hold — don't publish yet","description":"Leave it on PR #1 for human review/merge first. Onboard the first user via branch-install + key in the meantime (works today)."}],"multiSelect":false}]}

{"questions":[{"question":"GitHub Packages requires the package scope to match the repo owner, so @hosaka/crate can't go there as-is. Where + under what name should it publish?","header":"Registry/scope","options":[{"label":"@hosaka-fm/crate on GitHub Packages","description":"Rename scope to match the repo owner (hosaka-fm); publishes to GitHub Packages. Keeps it org-owned; egg-on is just the publisher. Least friction for 'GitHub's npm'."},{"label":"@egg-on/crate on GitHub Packages","description":"Publish under egg-on's own namespace — most dissociated from hosaka/jani. Needs the package owned by egg-on (egg-on repo or egg-on as scope)."},{"label":"@hosaka/crate on public npmjs","description":"Keep the locked name; publish to public npm under egg-on's npm account instead of GitHub Packages."}],"multiSelect":false},{"question":"How much identity to scrub from the published package? (The functional API host crate.0xhoneyjar.xyz stays either way — it's the endpoint the SDK calls.)","header":"Scrub level","options":[{"label":"Personal only (recommended)","description":"Package is already jani-free; just switch git authorship to egg-on. Keep @hosaka scope + 'The Honey Jar' LICENSE + the API host."},{"label":"Also neutralize brand","description":"Change LICENSE copyright (The Honey Jar → egg-on/neutral), reword README 'hosaka fleet', and rename the internal error-brand symbol. (API host still required.)"}],"multiSelect":false},{"question":"Every existing commit is authored by jani <jani@0xhoneyjar.xyz>. What about git history?","header":"Git history","options":[{"label":"egg-on going forward only","description":"Leave the 5 existing commits; author only new commits (merge + any scrub) as egg-on. No force-push."},{"label":"Rewrite all commits to egg-on","description":"Rewrite the branch's commit authorship to egg-on and force-push — removes jani from history entirely. More invasive."}],"multiSelect":false}]}

{"questions":[{"question":"Which npm scope/org handle do you want? (This becomes the permanent package name + the npm org you'll create. The display attribution is 'Hosaka FM' either way.)","header":"npm scope","options":[{"label":"@hosaka (recommended)","description":"Reverts to the originally-locked @hosaka/crate. Cleanest handle. Requires the 'hosaka' npm org to be available — confirmed when you run npm org create."},{"label":"@hosaka-fm","description":"Keeps the current @hosaka-fm/crate name (matches the 'Hosaka FM' org literally + the GitHub org hosaka-fm). Use if 'hosaka' is taken on npm or you prefer the exact match."}],"multiSelect":false},{"question":"How far should the 'jani' scrub go? (The shipped package + all SDK source/metadata are already 100% jani-free regardless.)","header":"jani scrub","options":[{"label":"SDK + metadata only","description":"Already done — nothing ships with jani. Leave internal Loa logs (grimoires/.beads) and the third-party .loa submodule as-is. Recommended."},{"label":"Also purge internal logs","description":"Additionally scrub jani from grimoires/ and .beads/ working-state logs (rewrite the old git-author mentions). More churn in non-shipped files."}],"multiSelect":false}]}

{"questions":[{"question":"The domain is wildcarded to Vercel and the obvious docs subdomains are already live Vercel sites. How should I deploy the crate docs site so you can see it?","header":"Deploy target","options":[{"label":"Vercel (recommended)","description":"Deploy the static Astro build to Vercel — matches your existing infra. HTTPS + CAA already handled by Vercel. I'd authenticate via the Vercel integration and deploy, then attach a subdomain. Cleanest fit; no production DNS/CAA overrides."},{"label":"AWS CloudFront (as you asked)","description":"Finish the AWS stack I started (S3+CloudFront+ACM). I'd create one explicit Route 53 record overriding the Vercel wildcard for a chosen subdomain, plus an amazon.com CAA at that name. More moving parts; touches production DNS."},{"label":"Hand me the build + instructions","description":"I tear down the AWS artifacts, give you the built site (site/dist) + a deploy recipe, and you wire the subdomain yourself."}],"multiSelect":false},{"question":"Which subdomain should the docs live at? (docs.0xhoneyjar.xyz and docs.crate.0xhoneyjar.xyz are already taken by Vercel sites.)","header":"Subdomain","options":[{"label":"crate-sdk.0xhoneyjar.xyz","description":"Clear, dedicated name for the @hosaka-fm/crate SDK docs."},{"label":"docs.crate.0xhoneyjar.xyz (reuse)","description":"If the existing Vercel site there is a placeholder you intend for these docs, deploy into that project instead of creating a new name."},{"label":"crate-docs.0xhoneyjar.xyz","description":"Alternative dedicated name."}],"multiSelect":false}]}

{"questions":[{"question":"How should we land the public release? (The export is baked for the slug hosaka-fm/crate-sdk, currently occupied by the private dev repo whose 17 PRs contain brand/PII.)","header":"Target repo","options":[{"label":"Rename dev repo → fresh public crate-sdk","description":"Rename private hosaka-fm/crate-sdk → hosaka-fm/crate-sdk-dev (keeps history+PRs private; GitHub auto-redirects old refs), then create a FRESH public hosaka-fm/crate-sdk and push the clean-room export. All ~40 baked URLs stay valid; zero history/PR leak. Preserves dev history in the renamed private repo."},{"label":"New public repo at a different slug","description":"Leave the private repo as-is; create a public repo at a new name (e.g. hosaka-fm/crate-client or crate-sdk-ts). I rewrite the ~40 baked hosaka-fm/crate-sdk references to the new slug before pushing. More surgery; diverges from the crate-sdk identity."},{"label":"Force-replace current repo + make public","description":"⚠ NOT recommended: making the existing hosaka-fm/crate-sdk public exposes all 17 closed PRs (titles, diffs, commits) which contain 0xhoneyjar/brand terms — force-pushing main cannot remove them. Fails the zero-mentions guarantee."}],"multiSelect":false},{"question":"Should the public repo auto-deploy the docs site to crate-sdk.hosaka.fm? (The export ships NO deploy workflow today — the private one had AWS infra literals.)","header":"Docs deploy","options":[{"label":"Defer — no deploy workflow yet","description":"Ship the public repo without docs-deploy.yml. Docs keep serving from the current AWS CloudFront (content persists). Cleaner: avoids committing infra AND the unresolved ABC Schengen font license — the trial webfont can't legally ship on a public build. Add a genericized workflow pre-launch once the font is licensed."},{"label":"Add genericized docs-deploy now","description":"I add docs-deploy.yml using repo secrets/vars for AWS account/OIDC-role/bucket/distribution (no literals committed). You must then set those secrets, confirm the OIDC trust matches the new repo path, and license the font before a public build can succeed."}],"multiSelect":false},{"question":"When we push, who should be the GitHub actor/repo-creator? (The active gh account here is 'deep-name', a brand-adjacent handle; 'egg-on' is not authenticated in this session. Commit author is already egg-on regardless.)","header":"Push identity","options":[{"label":"You push yourself under egg-on","description":"I prepare the finalized commit + exact gh/git commands and hand them off; you execute under the egg-on account. Nothing outward-facing happens from this session. Safest for attribution."},{"label":"I push via the deep-name token after your go","description":"After explicit confirmation, I create the repo + push using the authenticated deep-name token (it has admin:org + repo + workflow scopes). Commit author stays egg-on, but the GitHub actor/repo-creator is recorded as deep-name."}],"multiSelect":false}]}

{"questions":[{"question":"The clean-room release is verified and sitting on the private hosaka-fm/crate-sdk. Make it public now?","header":"Go public?","options":[{"label":"Public now + set repo metadata","description":"I run: gh repo edit hosaka-fm/crate-sdk --visibility public --accept-visibility-change-consequences, and set a clean description (\"Official typed TypeScript client for the crate public API — cluster-first, agent-native, zero dependencies\"), homepage https://crate-sdk.hosaka.fm, and topics (typescript, sdk, api-client, music-metadata, openapi, agent-friendly). Polished public landing."},{"label":"Public now, visibility only","description":"Just flip visibility to public; leave description/homepage/topics empty for you to set later."},{"label":"Hold — leave it private","description":"Do nothing outward-facing. The repo stays private for you to review and flip yourself. I stop here."}],"multiSelect":false}]}
